span.p: Use accident-year loss ratio - trended and developed - not calendar year

div.style: display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p: Use calendar-year combined ratio - but show prior-year development separately

div.style: display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p: Use ultimate loss ratio from development triangle - not incurred to date

div.style: display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p: Strip cat - strip prior-year development - use attritional current-year loss ratio

div.style: display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p: Use probabilistic cat model output - not the loss ratio

div.style: display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p: Apply trend factors - use indicated rate change formula

span.p: Also check LOB mix - a clean attritional ratio can mask a mix shift toward higher-risk lines
